chief_complaint: A 42-year-old woman presents with an acute exacerbation of hyperthyroidism, including headaches, increased bilateral eye pressure, fatigue, and heart palpitations, 3 weeks after a diagnosis of COVID-19.
history: The patient has a history of Graves' disease and recently tested positive for SARS-CoV-2. She reports daily headaches, increased bilateral eye pressure, fatigue, muscle weakness, episodes of confusion and agitation, and persistent heart palpitations.
exam: Not explicitly stated, but symptoms include goiter, episodes of confusion and agitation, and persistent heart palpitations.
labs: Elevated free T4 of 5.57, free T3 of 15.68, total T3 of 4.43, and near-absent thyroid stimulating hormone. The Burch-Wartofsky scale was 40.
correct_diagnosis: Graves' disease exacerbation with impending thyroid storm
differentials: Graves' disease exacerbation with impending thyroid storm
Thyroid crisis due to other causes
correct_ranking: Graves' disease exacerbation with impending thyroid storm
Thyroid crisis due to other causes
